finding_results.notes: The back half now discovers a new cost: effective sabotage requires spending punctuality margin and amending Nita's earlier no-lateness rule into 'late, not lost.' The closing exchange returns to that altered stake instead of ending on a fresh label alone.
finding_results.summary: The second scene now escalates beyond taxonomy by forcing a morally uglier operating rule and making the ending hinge on that sacrifice.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:257, chapters/chapter_10.md:275, chapters/chapter_10.md:297, chapters/chapter_10.md:393, chapters/chapter_10.md:517, chapters/chapter_10.md:539
finding_results.notes: The planning spans are now anchored in physical objects and leverage shifts: the lunch calendar, buzzing phone, school badge, leaking pen, folded route map, and wrist-grab all change how the rules are understood in scene.
finding_results.summary: List-heavy strategy talk was converted into embodied disagreement so the planning stays under live domestic and bodily pressure.

summary: Revised chapter 10 to compress repetitive planning taxonomy, dramatize the sabotage logic through objects and conflict, and end on the newly discovered cost of 'late, not lost' while preserving the chapter's paper-based conspiracy texture and continuity into chapter 11.
